children.text: Your intelligence is something very basic about you that you can’t change very much.
No matter what your intelligence level is, you can always increase it.
You can always substantially change how intelligent you are.
You are a certain kind of person, and you cannot really change that.
You can always change basic qualities that define the kind of person you are.
Music talent can be learned by anyone.
Only a few people are truly good at sports – you have to be born with athletic skills.
Math is much easier to learn if you are male or if you come from a culture that values math.
The harder you work at any skill, the better you will be at it.
No matter what kind of person you are, you can always change substantially.
Trying new things is stressful for me, and I avoid it.
Some people are good and kind, and some are not – it’s not often that people change.
I appreciate when parents, coaches, teachers give me feedback about my performance.
I often get angry when I get feedback about my performance.
All human beings without a brain injury or birth defect are capable of the same amount of learning.
You can learn new things, but you can’t really change how intelligent you are.
You can do things differently, but the important parts of who you are can’t really be changed.
Human beings are basically good, but sometimes make terrible decisions.
An important reason why I do my school work is that I like to learn new things.
Truly smart people do not need to try hard.
